g.text: These envelope fields are omitted here for clarity except
where transactionId is contextually relevant, e.g., the
periodic meter value events in the charge/discharge loops.
Provide Available PmaxSchedule or Tariff Table
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Started, chargingState = EVConnected, triggerReason = CablePluggedIn,
defaults to charging only -- must be
present for CS to offer BPT services.
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, chargingState = EVConnected, triggerReason = Authorized)
EVSENominalVoltage per Table 206 is the line-to-neutral
voltage. For US residential 240V split-phase (L1-L2 wiring
with no neutral on the EV side), implementations commonly
report 240 here so the EV can derive current directly from
EVMaxPower / 240V; a strict-spec reading would be 120
(L-N from either leg) with the EV computing 240V line-to-line.
Connector: SinglePhase matches numberPhases: 1 used in
AC has NO current/voltage fields (those are DC-only):
AC uses Power values (Watts), with EVSENominalVoltage
The rule applies to all parameters used for
discharging the EV battery, on both EV and EVSE sides.
Schedule Exchange (Dynamic Mode) and CSMS Charging Profile
V2X energy bounds for BPT negotiation.
Per Q01.FR.09, a strict implementation first requests
then renegotiates to AC_BPT after CSMS confirms BPT
(NOT ACChargingParametersType which is the ISO 15118-2 type).
magnitudes per the V2X schema; sign is conveyed by
the operationMode/setpoint, not by these limits.
(per K19.FR.05, CS does NOT wait; it returns
- CSMS controls power direction in real-time
- Responds to home load changes and solar production
[Until SetChargingProfileRequest received (CS sends ScheduleExchangeRes EVSEProcessing=Ongoing while waiting; CSMS SHOULD respond within 60s [K19.FR.08])]
SetChargingProfileRequest(evseId, chargingProfile(chargingProfilePurpose: TxProfile, transactionId, chargingProfileKind: Dynamic,
has no setpoint). Per Q03.FR.02, limit/dischargeLimit are
negative prices represent export earnings (EV -> home).
PriceLevelSchedule is an alternative for simpler
implementations but cannot express bidirectional pricing.
Per K19.FR.09, the CS returns the optional price schedule
from CSMS to the EV here.
per [V2G20-1065] it is required only when the
BPT system needs HLC-based channel switching
(this diagram), it is optional and omitted.
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, chargingState = Charging, triggerReason = ChargingStateChanged)
Per K19.FR.10, the CS SHALL disregard the EV's
EVPowerProfile and instead send the CSMS-supplied
chargingSchedule back to the CSMS, with
selectedChargingScheduleId set to the Id of the
chosen schedule. This applies in Dynamic Control Mode
Per [V2G20-1083], the SECC triggers the MeteringConfirmation
exchange by including MeterInfo in an AC_ChargeLoopRes and
the MeteringConfirmationReq/Res below travels on the side stream
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, triggerReason = MeterValuePeriodic, transactionInfo(transactionId), meterValue[](
Mid-Session Direction Switch and Discharging Phase (EV -> Home, -9.6kW)
CSMS switches EV from charging to V2H discharge
Direction change is implicit via negative setpoint.
CSMS already knows - it initiated the switch.
Per [V2G20-1083], the SECC triggers the MeteringConfirmation
exchange by including MeterInfo in an AC_ChargeLoopRes and
the MeteringConfirmationReq/Res below travels on the side stream
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, triggerReason = MeterValuePeriodic, transactionInfo(transactionId), meterValue[](
CSMS can switch direction at any time
End Charging, V2H Settlement, and Stop OCPP Transaction
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, chargingState = EVConnected, triggerReason = ChargingStateChanged)
Net V2H settlement: -$3.40 (credit to EV owner)
Derivation from final meter values below:
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Ended, chargingState = Idle, triggerReason = EVDeparted, meterValue[](
both Import and Export registers for billing.
